title: mentha × verticillata f. ballotifolia
intro: Mentha × verticillata f. ballotifolia, also known as peppermint, is a hybrid plant species of the mint family that is native to Europe and has been widely naturalized in other parts of the world. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to ease digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antibacterial agent. The health benefits of peppermint include relief from nausea, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to improve cognitive function and boost energy levels. Peppermint is often prepared as a tea, oil, or tincture, and can also be used as a flavoring agent in foods and beverages.
